question: Which customers have the highest spending on tracks from a single genre and what genre is it?
gold_sql:
WITH CustomerGenreSpending AS (
 SELECT
 c.customer_id,
 c.first_name,
 c.last_name,
 g.genre_id,
 g.name AS genre_name,
 SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_spending
 FROM customer c
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
 JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
 JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, g.genre_id, g.name
),
MaxSpendingPerCustomer AS (
 SELECT
 customer_id,
 MAX(total_spending) AS max_spending
 FROM CustomerGenreSpending
 GROUP BY customer_id
)
SELECT
 cgs.customer_id,
 cgs.first_name,
 cgs.last_name,
 cgs.genre_name,
 cgs.total_spending
FROM CustomerGenreSpending cgs
JOIN MaxSpendingPerCustomer mspc ON cgs.customer_id = mspc.customer_id AND cgs.total_spending = mspc.max_spending
ORDER BY cgs.total_spending DESC;

question: Which artists have the longest average track duration across all their albums?
gold_sql:
SELECT ar.artist_id, ar.name, AVG(t.milliseconds) AS avg_track_duration
FROM artist ar
JOIN album al ON ar.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY ar.artist_id, ar.name
ORDER BY avg_track_duration DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: How many tracks fall into each media type category, and what is the average duration for those tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT mt.name AS media_type, COUNT(t.track_id) AS track_count, AVG(t.milliseconds) AS avg_duration_milliseconds
FROM track t
JOIN media_type mt ON t.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id
GROUP BY mt.name
ORDER BY mt.name;

question: What is the distribution of tracks by genre for each artist?
gold_sql:
SELECT a.name AS artist_name, g.name AS genre_name, COUNT(t.track_id) AS track_count,
       COUNT(t.track_id) * 1.0 / SUM(COUNT(t.track_id)) OVER (PARTITION BY a.artist_id) AS track_percentage
FROM artist a
JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name, g.genre_id, g.name
ORDER BY a.name, g.name;

question: Show the details of invoices that include tracks from more than one genre.
gold_sql:
SELECT i.invoice_id, i.customer_id, i.invoice_date, i.billing_address, i.billing_city, i.billing_state, i.billing_country, i.billing_postal_code, i.total
FROM invoice i
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY i.invoice_id, i.customer_id, i.invoice_date, i.billing_address, i.billing_city, i.billing_state, i.billing_country, i.billing_postal_code, i.total
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) > 1;

question: What is the total duration of tracks by each media type, and which media type has the longest total duration?
gold_sql:
WITH MediaTypeDurations AS (
    SELECT mt.media_type_id, mt.name AS media_type_name, SUM(t.milliseconds) AS total_duration_milliseconds
    FROM media_type mt
    JOIN track t ON mt.media_type_id = t.media_type_id
    GROUP BY mt.media_type_id, mt.name
)
SELECT media_type_id, media_type_name, total_duration_milliseconds
FROM MediaTypeDurations
WHERE total_duration_milliseconds = (SELECT MAX(total_duration_milliseconds) FROM MediaTypeDurations);

question: Which employees have no direct reports but support customers with the highest total spending?
gold_sql:
WITH EmployeeDirectReports AS (
 SELECT e.employee_id
 FROM employee e
 LEFT JOIN employee r ON e.employee_id = r.reports_to
 -- North America countries
 GROUP BY e.employee_id
 HAVING COUNT(r.employee_id) = 0
),
EmployeeCustomerSpending AS (
 SELECT c.support_rep_id AS employee_id, SUM(i.total) AS total_spending
 FROM customer c
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 WHERE c.support_rep_id IS NOT NULL
 GROUP BY c.support_rep_id
),
MaxSpending AS (
 SELECT MAX(total_spending) AS max_spending
 FROM EmployeeCustomerSpending
)
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, ecs.total_spending
FROM employee e
JOIN EmployeeDirectReports edr ON e.employee_id = edr.employee_id
JOIN EmployeeCustomerSpending ecs ON e.employee_id = ecs.employee_id
JOIN MaxSpending ms ON ecs.total_spending = ms.max_spending
 ORDER BY e.employee_id;

question: What is the total number of tracks in the database?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_tracks FROM track;

question: List the albums that have generated the highest total revenue in the last quarter.
gold_sql:
SELECT a.album_id, a.title, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_revenue
FROM album a
JOIN track t ON a.album_id = t.album_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON t.track_id = il.track_id
JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
WHERE i.invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '3 months'
GROUP BY a.album_id, a.title
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Identify the top 10 cities by total invoice revenue generated.
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_city, SUM(total) AS total_revenue
FROM invoice
 GROUP BY billing_city
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: List albums that contain tracks available in multiple media types and specify the count of those media types.
gold_sql:
SELECT a.album_id, COUNT(DISTINCT t.media_type_id) AS media_type_count
FROM track t
JOIN album a ON t.album_id = a.album_id
GROUP BY a.album_id
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.media_type_id) > 1;

question: Which cities have employees with the highest average tenure and how does that correlate with total sales handled by their supported customers?
gold_sql:
SELECT
 e.city,
 AVG(EXTRACT(DAY FROM (CURRENT_DATE - e.hire_date))) / 365 AS avg_tenure_years,
 SUM(i.total) AS total_sales
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.city
ORDER BY avg_tenure_years DESC;

question: Which employees have support customers with invoice totals consistently above the company median?
gold_sql:
WITH CompanyMedian AS (
 SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY total) AS median_total
 FROM invoice
 ),
CustomerInvoiceTotals AS (
 SELECT c.support_rep_id, c.customer_id, SUM(i.total) AS total_invoice_amount
 FROM customer c
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY c.support_rep_id, c.customer_id
),
CustomerAboveMedian AS (
 SELECT support_rep_id, customer_id
 FROM CustomerInvoiceTotals, CompanyMedian
 WHERE total_invoice_amount > median_total
),
SupportRepCustomerCounts AS (
 SELECT support_rep_id, COUNT(customer_id) AS total_customers
 FROM CustomerInvoiceTotals
 GROUP BY support_rep_id
),
SupportRepAboveMedianCounts AS (
 SELECT support_rep_id, COUNT(customer_id) AS customers_above_median
 FROM CustomerAboveMedian
 GROUP BY support_rep_id
)
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name,
 COALESCE(srac.customers_above_median, 0)::float / srac2.total_customers AS pct_customers_above_median
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN SupportRepAboveMedianCounts srac ON e.employee_id = srac.support_rep_id
LEFT JOIN SupportRepCustomerCounts srac2 ON e.employee_id = srac2.support_rep_id
WHERE srac2.total_customers > 0
 AND COALESCE(srac.customers_above_median, 0) = srac2.total_customers
 AND e.title LIKE '%Support%'
ORDER BY pct_customers_above_median DESC;

question: Identify the top 3 artists whose albums have the highest total sales.
gold_sql:
SELECT a.name AS artist_name, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_sales
FROM artist a
JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON t.track_id = il.track_id
GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name
ORDER BY total_sales DESC
LIMIT 3;

question: Find the playlists that contain tracks all composed by the same composer.
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.composer) = 1;

question: How many tracks does each artist have in each genre, and which genre is most prevalent per artist?
gold_sql:
WITH ArtistGenreTrackCount AS (
  SELECT
    ar.artist_id,
    ar.name AS artist_name,
    g.genre_id,
    g.name AS genre_name,
    COUNT(t.track_id) AS track_count
  FROM artist ar
  JOIN album al ON ar.artist_id = al.artist_id
  JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
  JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
  GROUP BY ar.artist_id, ar.name, g.genre_id, g.name
),
ArtistMaxGenre AS (
  SELECT
    artist_id,
    MAX(track_count) AS max_track_count
  FROM ArtistGenreTrackCount
  GROUP BY artist_id
)
SELECT
  agtc.artist_id,
  agtc.artist_name,
  agtc.genre_id,
  agtc.genre_name,
  agtc.track_count,
  CASE WHEN agtc.track_count = amg.max_track_count THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_most_prevalent_genre
FROM ArtistGenreTrackCount agtc
JOIN ArtistMaxGenre amg ON agtc.artist_id = amg.artist_id
ORDER BY agtc.artist_id, agtc.genre_id;

question: Show all tracks with their album and artist names
gold_sql:
SELECT t.name as track_name, al.title as album_title, ar.name as artist_name
            FROM track t
            JOIN album al ON t.album_id = al.album_id
            JOIN artist ar ON al.artist_id = ar.artist_id

question: What is the breakdown of track counts and total invoice amounts for customers grouped by billing country?
gold_sql:
SELECT
  i.billing_country,
  COUNT(il.track_id) AS track_count,
  SUM(i.total) AS total_invoice_amount
FROM invoice i
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
WHERE i.billing_country IN ('USA', 'Canada', 'Mexico')
GROUP BY i.billing_country
ORDER BY i.billing_country;

question: Which playlists have an average track unit price higher than the average track price in the database?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.name AS playlist_name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING AVG(t.unit_price) > (SELECT AVG(unit_price) FROM track);

question: Which artists have more than 10 albums?
gold_sql:
SELECT ar.name, COUNT(*) as album_count
            FROM album al
            JOIN artist ar ON al.artist_id = ar.artist_id
            GROUP BY ar.artist_id, ar.name
            HAVING COUNT(*) > 10
            ORDER BY album_count DESC

question: List the customers who have bought tracks from artists with more than five albums.
gold_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN album a ON t.album_id = a.album_id
JOIN artist ar ON a.artist_id = ar.artist_id
WHERE ar.artist_id IN (
    SELECT artist_id
    FROM album
    GROUP BY artist_id
    HAVING COUNT(album_id) > 5
);

question: How many tracks by a particular artist appear in each playlist?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name AS playlist_name, a.artist_id, a.name AS artist_name, COUNT(pt.track_id) AS track_count
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN album al ON t.album_id = al.album_id
JOIN artist a ON al.artist_id = a.artist_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name, a.artist_id, a.name
ORDER BY p.playlist_id, a.artist_id;

question: Which customers have increased their monthly spending consistently over the past year?
gold_sql:
WITH MonthlySpending AS (
 SELECT
 customer_id,
 DATE_TRUNC('month', invoice_date) AS month,
 SUM(total) AS total_spent
 FROM invoice
 WHERE invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
 GROUP BY customer_id, month
),
RankedSpending AS (
 SELECT
 customer_id,
 month,
 total_spent,
 LAG(total_spent) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY month) AS prev_month_spent
 FROM MonthlySpending
),
ConsistentIncrease AS (
 SELECT
 customer_id
 FROM RankedSpending
 WHERE prev_month_spent IS NOT NULL
 GROUP BY customer_id
 HAVING BOOL_AND(total_spent > prev_month_spent)
)
SELECT
 c.customer_id,
 c.first_name,
 c.last_name
FROM ConsistentIncrease ci
JOIN customer c ON ci.customer_id = c.customer_id
 ORDER BY c.customer_id;

question: What is the average track price per genre and how does it rank from highest to lowest?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, AVG(t.unit_price) AS avg_track_price
FROM track t
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY avg_track_price DESC;

question: List playlists that include tracks from albums released by artists who have a track in at least three different playlists.
gold_sql:
WITH ArtistPlaylists AS (
  SELECT ar.artist_id
  FROM artist ar
  JOIN album al ON ar.artist_id = al.artist_id
  JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
  JOIN playlist_track pt ON t.track_id = pt.track_id
  GROUP BY ar.artist_id
  HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT pt.playlist_id) >= 3
)
SELECT DISTINCT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN album al ON t.album_id = al.album_id
JOIN ArtistPlaylists ap ON al.artist_id = ap.artist_id
ORDER BY p.playlist_id;

question: List the top 5 employees ranked by the total revenue generated from their assigned customers.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, SUM(i.total) AS total_revenue
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: What is the total number of customers per support representative, ordered by descending customer count?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(c.customer_id) AS total_customers
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY total_customers DESC;

question: Can you identify the employees who act as support representatives for customers from more than three different countries?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.country) AS distinct_countries
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT c.country) > 3;

question: Which playlists feature the highest average track price?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, AVG(t.unit_price) AS avg_track_price
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
ORDER BY avg_track_price DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: What is the total number of customers supported by each employee and their average invoice total?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) AS total_customers, AVG(i.total) AS avg_invoice_total
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY total_customers DESC;

question: Which playlists have more tracks than the average number of tracks per playlist, and what is their average unit price?
gold_sql:
WITH PlaylistTrackCounts AS (
  SELECT playlist_id, COUNT(track_id) AS track_count
  FROM playlist_track
  GROUP BY playlist_id
),
AverageTrackCount AS (
  SELECT AVG(track_count) AS avg_track_count
  FROM PlaylistTrackCounts
)
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, 
       ptc.track_count, 
       AVG(t.unit_price) AS avg_unit_price
FROM playlist p
JOIN PlaylistTrackCounts ptc ON p.playlist_id = ptc.playlist_id
JOIN AverageTrackCount atc ON 1=1
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
WHERE ptc.track_count > atc.avg_track_count
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name, ptc.track_count
ORDER BY ptc.track_count DESC;

question: How many unique tracks are included in all playlists combined?
gold_sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT track_id) AS unique_tracks_in_playlists
FROM playlist_track;

question: Which artist has released the most albums and what is the average price of the tracks in those albums?
gold_sql:
SELECT a.name AS artist_name, AVG(t.unit_price) AS avg_track_price
FROM artist a
JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
WHERE a.artist_id = (
    SELECT artist_id
    FROM album
    GROUP BY artist_id
    ORDER BY COUNT(album_id) DESC
    LIMIT 1
)
GROUP BY a.name;

question: List all genres and the count of tracks available for each genre.
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, COUNT(t.track_id) AS track_count
FROM genre g
LEFT JOIN track t ON g.genre_id = t.genre_id
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY g.name;

question: Can you rank artists by the number of distinct genres covered in their albums?
gold_sql:
SELECT a.artist_id, a.name, COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) AS distinct_genre_count
FROM artist a
JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name
ORDER BY distinct_genre_count DESC;

question: How many invoices were created each year?
gold_sql:
SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM invoice_date) AS year, COUNT(invoice_id) AS invoice_count
FROM invoice
GROUP BY year
ORDER BY year;

question: Identify support reps who manage customers with the highest average invoice size.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, AVG(i.total) AS avg_invoice_size
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY avg_invoice_size DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: What is the total revenue generated from invoices per media type?
gold_sql:
SELECT mt.name AS media_type, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_revenue
FROM invoice_line il
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN media_type mt ON t.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id
JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
 GROUP BY mt.name
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC;

question: Report the top 5 playlists in terms of total invoice sales for tracks contained within them.
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name AS playlist_name, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_sales
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON pt.track_id = il.track_id
JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
 GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
ORDER BY total_sales DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: Which genres have the highest average track price and how do they rank?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, AVG(t.unit_price) AS avg_track_price
FROM track t
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY avg_track_price DESC;

question: What is the average duration of all tracks in the catalog?
gold_sql:
SELECT AVG(milliseconds) AS average_duration_milliseconds
FROM track;

question: Which genre has the longest average track length?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, AVG(t.milliseconds) AS avg_track_length_milliseconds
FROM genre g
JOIN track t ON g.genre_id = t.genre_id
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY avg_track_length_milliseconds DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: What is the total number of tracks and average duration per album?
gold_sql:
SELECT album.album_id, album.title, COUNT(track.track_id) AS total_tracks, AVG(track.milliseconds) AS avg_duration_milliseconds
FROM album album
LEFT JOIN track track ON album.album_id = track.album_id
GROUP BY album.album_id, album.title
ORDER BY album.album_id;

question: Which customers have purchased the highest total number of tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, SUM(il.quantity) AS total_tracks_purchased
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
ORDER BY total_tracks_purchased DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: Among customers from the United States, who spent above the 75th percentile in the last 12 months?
gold_sql:
WITH CustomerSpending AS (
  SELECT
    c.customer_id,
    c.first_name,
    c.last_name,
    SUM(i.total) AS total_spent
  FROM customer c
  JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
  WHERE c.country = 'USA'
    AND i.invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '12 months'
  GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
),
Percentile75 AS (
  SELECT
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY total_spent) AS p75
  FROM CustomerSpending
)
SELECT
  cs.customer_id,
  cs.first_name,
  cs.last_name,
  cs.total_spent
FROM CustomerSpending cs, Percentile75 p
WHERE cs.total_spent > p.p75
ORDER BY cs.total_spent DESC;

question: Which support representatives handle customers from the greatest number of distinct countries?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.country) AS distinct_countries_handled
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY distinct_countries_handled DESC;

question: List the total spending of customers grouped by their country of residence.
gold_sql:
SELECT c.country, SUM(i.total) AS total_spending
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
WHERE c.country IN ('USA', 'Canada', 'Mexico')
GROUP BY c.country;

question: Find the artists whose albums contain the highest number of distinct genres in their tracks.
gold_sql:
WITH ArtistAlbumGenreCount AS (
    SELECT
        ar.artist_id,
        ar.name AS artist_name,
        al.album_id,
        COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) AS distinct_genre_count
    FROM artist ar
    JOIN album al ON ar.artist_id = al.artist_id
    JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
    GROUP BY ar.artist_id, ar.name, al.album_id
),
MaxGenreCount AS (
    SELECT
        artist_id,
        MAX(distinct_genre_count) AS max_distinct_genres
    FROM ArtistAlbumGenreCount
    GROUP BY artist_id
)
SELECT
    aagc.artist_name,
    aagc.album_id,
    aagc.distinct_genre_count
FROM ArtistAlbumGenreCount aagc
JOIN MaxGenreCount mgc ON aagc.artist_id = mgc.artist_id AND aagc.distinct_genre_count = mgc.max_distinct_genres
ORDER BY aagc.distinct_genre_count DESC;

question: Which customers have purchased the most tracks by quantity and what is their total spending?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, SUM(il.quantity) AS total_tracks_purchased, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_spending
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
ORDER BY total_tracks_purchased DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: How many employees have a fax number listed in their contact information?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS employees_with_fax FROM employee WHERE fax IS NOT NULL AND fax <> ''

question: Find the playlists that include tracks longer than the average track duration across all playlists.
gold_sql:
WITH avg_duration AS (
  SELECT AVG(milliseconds) AS avg_milliseconds
  FROM track
),
playlist_long_tracks AS (
  SELECT pt.playlist_id
  FROM playlist_track pt
  JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
  JOIN avg_duration ad ON 1=1
  WHERE t.milliseconds > ad.avg_milliseconds
  GROUP BY pt.playlist_id
)
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_long_tracks plt ON p.playlist_id = plt.playlist_id;

question: What is the average invoice total amount?
gold_sql:
SELECT AVG(total) AS average_invoice_total_amount
FROM invoice

question: How many albums does each artist have, and what is the average number of tracks per album?
gold_sql:
SELECT a.artist_id, ar.name AS artist_name, COUNT(DISTINCT a.album_id) AS album_count, AVG(track_count) AS avg_tracks_per_album
FROM artist ar
JOIN album a ON ar.artist_id = a.artist_id
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT album_id, COUNT(track_id) AS track_count
    FROM track
    GROUP BY album_id
) t ON a.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY a.artist_id, ar.name
ORDER BY album_count DESC;

question: How many albums does each artist have, and what is the total number of tracks per artist?
gold_sql:
SELECT ar.artist_id, ar.name AS artist_name, COUNT(DISTINCT al.album_id) AS album_count, COUNT(t.track_id) AS total_tracks
FROM artist ar
LEFT JOIN album al ON ar.artist_id = al.artist_id
LEFT JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY ar.artist_id, ar.name
ORDER BY ar.artist_id;

question: What is the total quantity of invoice lines sold in Canada?
gold_sql:
SELECT SUM(il.quantity) AS total_quantity_sold_in_canada
FROM invoice_line il
JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
WHERE i.billing_country = 'Canada';

question: What is the total sales amount grouped by month and genre for the last year?
gold_sql:
SELECT
 TO_CHAR(i.invoice_date, 'YYYY-MM') AS year_month,
 g.name AS genre,
 SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_sales_amount
FROM invoice_line il
JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE i.invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
 -- Assuming North America region is represented by USA
GROUP BY year_month, genre
ORDER BY year_month, genre;

question: List all tracks that have no composer listed
gold_sql: SELECT name FROM track WHERE composer IS NULL

question: What is the total spending of each customer by country?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.country, c.customer_id, SUM(i.total) AS total_spending
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
WHERE c.country IN ('USA', 'Canada', 'Mexico')
GROUP BY c.country, c.customer_id
ORDER BY c.country, total_spending DESC;

question: What are the top five billing cities by total invoice amount in the last quarter?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_city, SUM(total) AS total_invoice_amount
FROM invoice
WHERE invoice_date >= DATE_TRUNC('quarter', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '3 months'
  AND invoice_date < DATE_TRUNC('quarter', CURRENT_DATE)
GROUP BY billing_city
ORDER BY total_invoice_amount DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: How many playlists have exactly 10 tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS playlists_with_10_tracks
FROM (
    SELECT playlist_id, COUNT(track_id) AS track_count
    FROM playlist_track
    GROUP BY playlist_id
    HAVING COUNT(track_id) = 10
) AS subquery;

question: What is the total number of playlists in the database?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_playlists FROM playlist;

question: Which artists have albums with more than 15 tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT ar.name AS artist_name
FROM artist ar
JOIN album al ON ar.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track tr ON al.album_id = tr.album_id
GROUP BY ar.artist_id, ar.name
HAVING COUNT(tr.track_id) > 15;

question: Which tracks are priced above the average track price?
gold_sql:
SELECT name, unit_price
            FROM track
            WHERE unit_price > (SELECT AVG(unit_price) FROM track)
            ORDER BY unit_price DESC

question: What is the average invoice total by billing state, and which states have significantly higher averages than the national average?
gold_sql:
WITH StateAvg AS (
  SELECT
    billing_state,
    AVG(total) AS avg_invoice_total
  FROM invoice
  WHERE billing_country IN ('USA', 'Canada', 'Mexico')
  GROUP BY billing_state
),
NationalAvg AS (
  SELECT AVG(total) AS national_avg_invoice_total
  FROM invoice
  WHERE billing_country IN ('USA', 'Canada', 'Mexico')
)
SELECT
  s.billing_state,
  s.avg_invoice_total,
  n.national_avg_invoice_total,
  CASE
    WHEN s.avg_invoice_total > n.national_avg_invoice_total THEN 1
    ELSE 0
  END AS higher_than_national_avg
FROM StateAvg s
CROSS JOIN NationalAvg n
ORDER BY s.avg_invoice_total DESC;

question: What is the average track duration for each genre and how do they compare in terms of total track count?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name,
       AVG(t.milliseconds) / 1000.0 AS avg_track_duration_seconds,
       COUNT(t.track_id) AS total_track_count
FROM track t
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY avg_track_duration_seconds DESC;

question: Rank employees by the total number of customers assigned and the average invoice total of those customers.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name,
 COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) AS total_customers,
 AVG(i.total) AS avg_invoice_total
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY total_customers DESC, avg_invoice_total DESC;

question: How does the monthly revenue trend differ for customers in different states over the past six months?
gold_sql:
SELECT
  to_char(invoice_date, 'YYYY-MM') AS year_month,
  billing_state AS state,
  SUM(total) AS monthly_revenue
FROM invoice
WHERE invoice_date >= current_date - interval '6 months'
GROUP BY year_month, state
ORDER BY year_month, state;

question: Can you show the total spending of each customer along with the number of invoices they have?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, COUNT(i.invoice_id) AS number_of_invoices, SUM(i.total) AS total_spending
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
ORDER BY total_spending DESC;

question: For each playlist, what is the percentage of tracks priced above the average track price in the database?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, 
       CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN t.unit_price > (SELECT AVG(unit_price) FROM track) THEN 1 END) AS FLOAT) / COUNT(t.track_id) AS percentage_above_avg_price
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
ORDER BY p.playlist_id;

question: What is the average invoice total by billing country?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_country, ROUND(AVG(total), 2) as avg_total
            FROM invoice
            GROUP BY billing_country
            ORDER BY avg_total DESC

question: Identify the top 5 artists with the greatest total track sales (quantity) in the past year.
gold_sql:
SELECT a.artist_id, a.name AS artist_name, SUM(il.quantity) AS total_quantity
FROM artist a
JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON t.track_id = il.track_id
JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
WHERE i.invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name
ORDER BY total_quantity DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: How many tracks are there in each playlist?
gold_sql:
SELECT pt.playlist_id, p.name AS playlist_name, COUNT(pt.track_id) AS track_count
FROM playlist_track pt
JOIN playlist p ON pt.playlist_id = p.playlist_id
GROUP BY pt.playlist_id, p.name
ORDER BY pt.playlist_id;

question: List the playlists that contain tracks exclusively from one genre and have more than 10 tracks.
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) = 1
   AND COUNT(pt.track_id) > 10;

question: Which month recorded the highest total invoice amount for customers in the United States over the past two years?
gold_sql:
SELECT
  TO_CHAR(invoice.invoice_date, 'YYYY-MM') AS year_month,
  SUM(invoice.total) AS total_invoice_amount
FROM invoice
JOIN customer ON invoice.customer_id = customer.customer_id
WHERE customer.country = 'USA'
  AND invoice.invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '2 years'
GROUP BY year_month
ORDER BY total_invoice_amount DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which billing cities have shown consistent growth in invoice totals for the past three consecutive quarters?
gold_sql:
WITH QuarterlyTotals AS (
 SELECT
 billing_city,
 DATE_TRUNC('quarter', invoice_date) AS quarter,
 SUM(total) AS total_invoice_amount
 FROM invoice
 -- North America region
 GROUP BY billing_city, quarter
),
RankedQuarters AS (
 SELECT
 billing_city,
 quarter,
 total_invoice_amount,
 LAG(total_invoice_amount, 1) OVER (PARTITION BY billing_city ORDER BY quarter) AS prev_quarter_1,
 LAG(total_invoice_amount, 2) OVER (PARTITION BY billing_city ORDER BY quarter) AS prev_quarter_2
 FROM QuarterlyTotals
)
SELECT DISTINCT billing_city
FROM RankedQuarters
WHERE total_invoice_amount > prev_quarter_1
 AND prev_quarter_1 > prev_quarter_2
 AND prev_quarter_1 IS NOT NULL
 AND prev_quarter_2 IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY billing_city;

question: How many playlists contain tracks exceeding 5 minutes in length?
gold_sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT pt.playlist_id) AS playlists_with_long_tracks
FROM playlist_track pt
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
WHERE t.milliseconds > 300000;

question: Which support representative manages customers with the largest variation in monthly invoice totals?
gold_sql:
WITH MonthlyTotals AS (
 SELECT
 c.support_rep_id,
 c.customer_id,
 DATE_TRUNC('month', i.invoice_date) AS invoice_month,
 SUM(i.total) AS monthly_total
 FROM customer c
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 -- North America countries
 GROUP BY c.support_rep_id, c.customer_id, invoice_month
),
CustomerVariation AS (
 SELECT
 support_rep_id,
 customer_id,
 STDDEV(monthly_total) AS monthly_total_variation
 FROM MonthlyTotals
 GROUP BY support_rep_id, customer_id
),
SupportRepVariation AS (
 SELECT
 support_rep_id,
 AVG(monthly_total_variation) AS avg_variation
 FROM CustomerVariation
 GROUP BY support_rep_id
)
SELECT
 e.employee_id AS support_rep_id,
 e.first_name,
 e.last_name,
 s.avg_variation
FROM SupportRepVariation s
JOIN employee e ON s.support_rep_id = e.employee_id
ORDER BY s.avg_variation DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which customers have purchased tracks from more than five different genres?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) AS genre_count
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) > 5;

question: What is the total quantity of tracks purchased by customers in each country?
gold_sql:
SELECT 
    c.country,
    SUM(il.quantity) AS total_quantity
FROM 
    invoice_line il
JOIN 
    invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
JOIN 
    customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
GROUP BY 
    c.country
ORDER BY 
    total_quantity DESC;

question: How many playlists does each track appear in?
gold_sql:
SELECT pt.track_id, COUNT(pt.playlist_id) AS playlist_count
FROM playlist_track pt
GROUP BY pt.track_id
ORDER BY pt.track_id;

question: Can you list playlists along with the count of distinct artists featured in each?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name AS playlist_name, COUNT(DISTINCT ar.artist_id) AS distinct_artist_count
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN album al ON t.album_id = al.album_id
JOIN artist ar ON al.artist_id = ar.artist_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
ORDER BY p.playlist_id;

question: What is the average amount spent per invoice for customers in California?
What is the count of invoices per billing state?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_state, COUNT(invoice_id) AS invoice_count
FROM invoice
GROUP BY billing_state
ORDER BY invoice_count DESC;

question: Among customers with more than five invoices, who has the lowest average invoice total?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, AVG(i.total) AS avg_invoice_total
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
HAVING COUNT(i.invoice_id) > 5
ORDER BY avg_invoice_total ASC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which managers have teams with the highest combined customer spending?
gold_sql:
SELECT
 mgr.employee_id AS manager_id,
 mgr.first_name || ' ' || mgr.last_name AS manager_name,
 SUM(inv.total) AS total_customer_spending
FROM employee AS mgr
JOIN employee AS rep ON rep.reports_to = mgr.employee_id
JOIN customer AS cust ON cust.support_rep_id = rep.employee_id
JOIN invoice AS inv ON inv.customer_id = cust.customer_id
 GROUP BY mgr.employee_id, mgr.first_name, mgr.last_name
ORDER BY total_customer_spending DESC;

question: Can you identify the artists whose albums have a median track size above the overall median track size?
gold_sql:
WITH OverallMedian AS (
  SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY bytes) AS median_bytes
  FROM track
),
AlbumMedian AS (
  SELECT album_id, PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY bytes) AS median_bytes
  FROM track
  GROUP BY album_id
),
AlbumsAboveMedian AS (
  SELECT album_id
  FROM AlbumMedian, OverallMedian
  WHERE AlbumMedian.median_bytes > OverallMedian.median_bytes
)
SELECT DISTINCT artist.artist_id, artist.name
FROM artist
JOIN album ON artist.artist_id = album.artist_id
JOIN AlbumsAboveMedian ON album.album_id = AlbumsAboveMedian.album_id
ORDER BY artist.name;

question: Which media type has the most tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT mt.name AS media_type, COUNT(t.track_id) AS track_count
FROM media_type mt
JOIN track t ON mt.media_type_id = t.media_type_id
GROUP BY mt.name
ORDER BY track_count DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: For each genre, what is the median track duration and how many tracks fall below and above this median?
gold_sql:
WITH RankedTracks AS (
  SELECT
    g.genre_id,
    g.name AS genre_name,
    t.track_id,
    t.milliseconds,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY g.genre_id ORDER BY t.milliseconds) AS rn_asc,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY g.genre_id ORDER BY t.milliseconds DESC) AS rn_desc,
    COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY g.genre_id) AS total_tracks
  FROM track t
  JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
),
MedianTracks AS (
  SELECT
    genre_id,
    genre_name,
    AVG(median_val) AS median_duration
  FROM (
    SELECT
      genre_id,
      genre_name,
      milliseconds AS median_val
    FROM RankedTracks
    WHERE rn_asc = rn_desc OR rn_asc + 1 = rn_desc
  ) median_candidates
  GROUP BY genre_id, genre_name
),
TrackCounts AS (
  SELECT
    t.genre_id,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN t.milliseconds < m.median_duration THEN 1 END) AS below_median_count,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN t.milliseconds > m.median_duration THEN 1 END) AS above_median_count
  FROM track t
  JOIN MedianTracks m ON t.genre_id = m.genre_id
  GROUP BY t.genre_id
)
SELECT
  m.genre_name,
  m.median_duration,
  c.below_median_count,
  c.above_median_count
FROM MedianTracks m
JOIN TrackCounts c ON m.genre_id = c.genre_id
ORDER BY m.genre_name;

question: For each manager, show the number of direct reports and the cumulative invoice amounts generated by customers supported by those reports.
gold_sql:
SELECT
 mgr.employee_id AS manager_id,
 mgr.first_name || ' ' || mgr.last_name AS manager_name,
 COUNT(DISTINCT rep.employee_id) AS number_of_direct_reports,
 COALESCE(SUM(inv.total), 0) AS cumulative_invoice_amount
FROM employee AS mgr
LEFT JOIN employee AS rep ON rep.reports_to = mgr.employee_id
LEFT JOIN customer AS cust ON cust.support_rep_id = rep.employee_id
LEFT JOIN invoice AS inv ON inv.customer_id = cust.customer_id
 GROUP BY mgr.employee_id, mgr.first_name, mgr.last_name
ORDER BY mgr.employee_id;

question: How many tracks have a unit price above the average price?
List cities with total invoice amounts greater than the average invoice amount across all cities.
gold_sql:
WITH city_totals AS (
  SELECT billing_city, SUM(total) AS total_invoice_amount
  FROM invoice
  GROUP BY billing_city
),
avg_total AS (
  SELECT AVG(total_invoice_amount) AS avg_invoice_amount
  FROM city_totals
)
SELECT billing_city, total_invoice_amount
FROM city_totals
WHERE total_invoice_amount > (SELECT avg_invoice_amount FROM avg_total);

question: List the top three countries by total sales revenue and how their sales changed compared to the previous year.
gold_sql:
WITH yearly_sales AS (
  SELECT
    billing_country AS country,
    TO_CHAR(invoice_date, 'YYYY') AS year,
    SUM(total) AS total_revenue
  FROM invoice
  WHERE billing_country IN (SELECT DISTINCT country FROM customer WHERE country IS NOT NULL)
  GROUP BY billing_country, year
),
ranked_sales AS (
  SELECT
    country,
    year,
    total_revenue,
    RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY year ORDER BY total_revenue DESC) AS revenue_rank
  FROM yearly_sales
),
sales_with_change AS (
  SELECT
    current.country,
    current.year,
    current.total_revenue,
    previous.total_revenue AS previous_year_revenue,
    CASE
      WHEN previous.total_revenue IS NULL THEN NULL
      ELSE (current.total_revenue - previous.total_revenue) / previous.total_revenue::numeric
    END AS revenue_change
  FROM ranked_sales current
  LEFT JOIN ranked_sales previous
    ON current.country = previous.country
    AND current.year::integer = previous.year::integer + 1
  WHERE current.revenue_rank <= 3
)
SELECT
  country,
  year,
  total_revenue,
  previous_year_revenue,
  revenue_change
FROM sales_with_change
ORDER BY year DESC, total_revenue DESC;

question: For each artist, what is the total size in bytes of all their tracks combined, and who are the top 5 artists by this metric?
gold_sql:
SELECT a.artist_id, a.name AS artist_name, SUM(t.bytes) AS total_bytes
FROM artist a
JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name
ORDER BY total_bytes DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: What is the total spending of customers grouped by their assigned support representative?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, SUM(i.total) AS total_spending
FROM customer c
JOIN employee e ON c.support_rep_id = e.employee_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY total_spending DESC;

question: Find the total number of invoices and average invoice total by year.
gold_sql:
SELECT
  EXTRACT(YEAR FROM invoice_date) AS year,
  COUNT(invoice_id) AS total_invoices,
  AVG(total) AS average_invoice_total
FROM invoice
GROUP BY year
ORDER BY year;

question: How many tracks have a composer listed as 'Unknown'?
Which employees have been hired most recently and how many customers do they support?
gold_sql:
WITH RecentHires AS (
 SELECT employee_id, first_name, last_name, hire_date
 FROM employee
WHERE hire_date = (SELECT MAX(hire_date) FROM employee )
)
SELECT rh.employee_id, rh.first_name, rh.last_name, rh.hire_date, COUNT(c.customer_id) AS customer_count
FROM RecentHires rh
LEFT JOIN customer c ON rh.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
GROUP BY rh.employee_id, rh.first_name, rh.last_name, rh.hire_date
ORDER BY rh.employee_id;

question: List the playlists with fewer than 5 tracks.
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, COUNT(pt.track_id) AS track_count
FROM playlist p
LEFT JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(pt.track_id) < 5;

question: What are the top five cities with the highest average invoice total for customers buying tracks from the 'Rock' genre?
gold_sql:
SELECT i.billing_city AS city, AVG(i.total) AS avg_invoice_total
FROM invoice i
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
WHERE g.name = 'Rock'
 GROUP BY i.billing_city
ORDER BY avg_invoice_total DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: Which billing cities have seen an increase in total invoice amounts compared to the previous year?
gold_sql:
WITH yearly_totals AS (
 SELECT billing_city, 
 EXTRACT(YEAR FROM invoice_date) AS year, 
 SUM(total) AS total_invoice_amount
 FROM invoice
 GROUP BY billing_city, year
),

city_year_comparison AS (
 SELECT a.billing_city, a.year, a.total_invoice_amount, b.total_invoice_amount AS prev_year_amount
 FROM yearly_totals a
 LEFT JOIN yearly_totals b ON a.billing_city = b.billing_city AND a.year = b.year + 1
)

SELECT billing_city, year, total_invoice_amount, prev_year_amount,
 (total_invoice_amount - prev_year_amount) / prev_year_amount AS increase_percentage
FROM city_year_comparison
WHERE prev_year_amount IS NOT NULL
 AND total_invoice_amount > prev_year_amount
ORDER BY billing_city, year;

question: Which artists have released albums that include tracks exceeding the average track length for their genre?
gold_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT a.artist_id, a.name
FROM artist a
JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
JOIN (
    SELECT genre_id, AVG(milliseconds) AS avg_genre_length
    FROM track
    GROUP BY genre_id
) genre_avg ON t.genre_id = genre_avg.genre_id
WHERE t.milliseconds > genre_avg.avg_genre_length;

question: What is the sum of all invoice totals for customers in New York city?
gold_sql:
SELECT SUM(i.total) AS total_invoice_sum
FROM invoice i
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE c.city = 'New York'

question: How many invoices were created each month in 2022?
gold_sql:
SELECT
                EXTRACT(MONTH FROM invoice_date) as month,
                COUNT(*) as invoice_count
            FROM invoice
            WHERE invoice_date >= '2022-01-01' AND invoice_date < '2023-01-01'
            GROUP BY EXTRACT(MONTH FROM invoice_date)
            ORDER BY month

question: Which media type has the highest average track price?
gold_sql:
SELECT mt.name AS media_type, AVG(t.unit_price) AS avg_track_price
FROM track t
JOIN media_type mt ON t.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id
GROUP BY mt.name
ORDER BY avg_track_price DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which albums contain the highest number of tracks composed by a single composer?
gold_sql:
WITH ComposerTrackCounts AS (
    SELECT
        album_id,
        composer,
        COUNT(track_id) AS track_count
    FROM track
    WHERE composer IS NOT NULL AND composer <> ''
    GROUP BY album_id, composer
),
MaxComposerCounts AS (
    SELECT
        album_id,
        MAX(track_count) AS max_track_count
    FROM ComposerTrackCounts
    GROUP BY album_id
),
AlbumsWithMaxComposer AS (
    SELECT
        c.album_id,
        c.composer,
        c.track_count
    FROM ComposerTrackCounts c
    JOIN MaxComposerCounts m ON c.album_id = m.album_id AND c.track_count = m.max_track_count
)
SELECT
    a.title AS album_title,
    ac.composer,
    ac.track_count
FROM AlbumsWithMaxComposer ac
JOIN album a ON ac.album_id = a.album_id
ORDER BY ac.track_count DESC, a.title;

question: Which employees started working in the company before 2010?
Find all customers whose last name starts with 'S'
gold_sql:
SELECT first_name, last_name
            FROM customer
            WHERE last_name LIKE 'S%'
            ORDER BY last_name

question: For each employee, how many customers do they support and what is the total invoice amount generated?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name,
 COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) AS number_of_customers,
 COALESCE(SUM(i.total), 0) AS total_invoice_amount
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
LEFT JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY e.employee_id;

question: How does the total number of customers per employee correlate with the average invoice total for those customers?
gold_sql:
SELECT
 CORR(total_customers, avg_invoice_total) AS correlation
FROM (
 SELECT
 e.employee_id,
 COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) AS total_customers,
 AVG(i.total) AS avg_invoice_total
 FROM employee e
 JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id
) sub;

question: What is the total quantity of tracks sold by genre along with the total sales revenue for each genre?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, SUM(il.quantity) AS total_quantity_sold, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_sales_revenue
FROM invoice_line il
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY total_sales_revenue DESC;

question: Which employee has been with the company the longest?
gold_sql:
SELECT first_name, last_name, hire_date
FROM employee
ORDER BY hire_date
LIMIT 1;

question: What is the smallest track size in bytes?
gold_sql: SELECT MIN(bytes) AS smallest_track_size_bytes FROM track;

question: How many playlists include at least one track longer than 5 minutes?
gold_sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT pt.playlist_id) AS playlists_with_long_tracks
FROM playlist_track pt
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
WHERE t.milliseconds > 300000;

question: What is the total size in bytes of all tracks in the store?
gold_sql: SELECT SUM(bytes) AS total_bytes FROM track;

question: What is the average number of tracks per playlist, grouped by the playlist name initial letter?
gold_sql:
SELECT
  LEFT(p.name, 1) AS playlist_initial,
  AVG(track_count) AS avg_tracks_per_playlist
FROM
  playlist p
JOIN
  (SELECT playlist_id, COUNT(track_id) AS track_count
   FROM playlist_track
   GROUP BY playlist_id) pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
GROUP BY
  LEFT(p.name, 1)
ORDER BY
  playlist_initial;

question: What is the average unit price of tracks sold according to invoice lines?
gold_sql:
SELECT AVG(unit_price) AS average_unit_price_sold
FROM invoice_line;

question: For each customer, show their most recent invoice
gold_sql:
SELECT first_name, last_name, invoice_date, total FROM (
                SELECT
                    c.first_name, c.last_name,
                    i.invoice_date, i.total,
                    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
                        PARTITION BY c.customer_id
                        ORDER BY i.invoice_date DESC
                    ) as rn
                FROM customer c
                JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
            ) latest
            WHERE rn = 1
            ORDER BY last_name, first_name

question: What are the total number of invoices and total sales value by billing country?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_country, COUNT(invoice_id) AS total_invoices, SUM(total) AS total_sales_value
FROM invoice
GROUP BY billing_country
ORDER BY billing_country;

question: Identify playlists where track counts by genre differ significantly from the overall genre distribution across all tracks.
gold_sql:
WITH OverallGenreDist AS (
  SELECT
    genre_id,
    COUNT(*)::DECIMAL / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM track) AS overall_genre_pct
  FROM track
  GROUP BY genre_id
),
PlaylistGenreDist AS (
  SELECT
    pt.playlist_id,
    t.genre_id,
    COUNT(*)::DECIMAL / COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY pt.playlist_id) AS playlist_genre_pct
  FROM playlist_track pt
  JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
  GROUP BY pt.playlist_id, t.genre_id
),
GenreDiff AS (
  SELECT
    p.playlist_id,
    p.genre_id,
    p.playlist_genre_pct,
    o.overall_genre_pct,
    ABS(p.playlist_genre_pct - o.overall_genre_pct) AS diff
  FROM PlaylistGenreDist p
  JOIN OverallGenreDist o ON p.genre_id = o.genre_id
),
SignificantDiffPlaylists AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT playlist_id
  FROM GenreDiff
  WHERE diff > 0.1 -- threshold for significant difference
)
SELECT pl.playlist_id, pl.name
FROM playlist pl
JOIN SignificantDiffPlaylists sdp ON pl.playlist_id = sdp.playlist_id
ORDER BY pl.playlist_id;

question: What is the total number of customers residing in each city?
gold_sql:
SELECT city, COUNT(customer_id) AS total_customers
FROM customer
GROUP BY city
ORDER BY total_customers DESC;

question: Which employees manage customers who have collectively purchased tracks from the highest number of distinct artists?
gold_sql:
WITH EmployeeArtistCounts AS (
 SELECT
 e.employee_id,
 e.first_name,
 e.last_name,
 COUNT(DISTINCT ar.artist_id) AS distinct_artist_count
 FROM employee e
 JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
 JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
 JOIN album al ON t.album_id = al.album_id
 JOIN artist ar ON al.artist_id = ar.artist_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
), MaxArtistCount AS (
 SELECT MAX(distinct_artist_count) AS max_count FROM EmployeeArtistCounts
)
SELECT
 eac.employee_id,
 eac.first_name,
 eac.last_name,
 eac.distinct_artist_count
FROM EmployeeArtistCounts eac
JOIN MaxArtistCount mac ON eac.distinct_artist_count = mac.max_count
ORDER BY eac.employee_id;

question: What is the total revenue generated from invoices that include tracks from more than one genre?
gold_sql:
SELECT SUM(sub.total) AS total_revenue
FROM (
  SELECT i.invoice_id, i.total
  FROM invoice i
  JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
  JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
  GROUP BY i.invoice_id, i.total
  HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) > 1
) sub;

question: How many playlists include tracks priced in the top 10% by unit price?
gold_sql:
WITH PriceThreshold AS (
  SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.9) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY unit_price) AS top_10_percent_price
  FROM track
),
TopPricedTracks AS (
  SELECT track_id
  FROM track, PriceThreshold
  WHERE unit_price >= top_10_percent_price
),
PlaylistsWithTopPricedTracks AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT playlist_id
  FROM playlist_track
  WHERE track_id IN (SELECT track_id FROM TopPricedTracks)
)
SELECT COUNT(*) AS playlists_with_top_10_percent_priced_tracks
FROM PlaylistsWithTopPricedTracks;

question: What is the average duration of tracks in each playlist?
gold_sql:
SELECT pt.playlist_id, AVG(t.milliseconds) AS avg_duration_milliseconds
FROM playlist_track pt
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY pt.playlist_id;

question: Which customers have purchased tracks from more than three different genres?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) AS genre_count
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) > 3;

question: How many invoices were issued on weekends compared to weekdays?
gold_sql:
SELECT
 SUM(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(DOW FROM invoice_date) IN (0, 6) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS weekend_invoices,
 SUM(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(DOW FROM invoice_date) NOT IN (0, 6) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS weekday_invoices,
 ROUND(1.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(DOW FROM invoice_date) IN (0, 6) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 4) AS weekend_percentage,
 ROUND(1.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(DOW FROM invoice_date) NOT IN (0, 6) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 4) AS weekday_percentage
FROM invoice
